Classifications.Text: // File ulx3s_v20_passthru_wifi.vhd translated with vhd2vl v3.0 VHDL to Verilog RTL translator

a freshly converted 6a044e8 version from March 2
// see ht

hd2vl is Free (libre) Software:
//   Copyright (C) 2001 Vincenzo Liguori - Ocean Logic Pty Ltd

//   vhd2vl comes with ABSOLUTELY NO WARRANTY.  Always check the resulting
/
/ -- main clock input from 25MHz clock source must be lowercase
ut  wire ftdi_ndsr, // ERROR: IO 'ftdi_ndsr' is unconstrained in LPF
ut  wire wifi_gpio2, // ERROR: IO 'wifi_gpio2' is unconstrained in LPF
/ GPIO (some are shared with wifi and adc)
/ SHUTDOWN: logic '1' here will shutdown power on PCB >= v1.7.5
clock input from 25MHz clock source must be lowercase

ll disable wifi by default
// Onboa

O (some are shared with wifi and adc)
// SHUTDOWN: logic '1' here will shutdown power on PCB >= v1.7.5

this low will skip to next multiboot image


  assig
/ holding BTN0 will hold gpio0 LOW, signal for ESP32 to take control
0) <= '0' when wifi_gpio0 = '0' else 'Z'; -- gpio2 together with gpio0 to 0

ign sd_d[3] = R_prog_release[(C_prog_release_timeout)] == 1'b0 ? 1'b1 : 1'bZ; // sd_d(3) <= '1' when R_prog_release(R_prog_release'high) = '0' else 'Z';
	// assign sd_clk = R_prog_release[(C_prog_release_timeout)] == 1'b0 ? clk_25mhz : 1'bZ; // sd_clk <= clk_25mhz when R_prog_release(R_prog_release'high) = '0' else 'Z';

ssign wifi_en = ftdi_nrts; // wifi_en <= ftdi_nrts;
	// assign wifi_gpio0 = ftdi_ndtr; // wifi_gpio0 <= ftdi_ndtr;

ssign led[7:0] = {S_oled_csn,R_spi_miso[0],sd_clk,sd_d[2],sd_d[3],sd_cmd,sd_d[0],sd_d[1]}; // led(7 downto 0) <= S_oled_csn & R_spi_miso(0) & sd_clk & sd_d(2) & sd_d(3) & sd_cmd & sd_d(0) & sd_d(1); -- beautiful but makes core unreliable

	/

programming start: blinks too short to be visible

	// gr

n LED indicates ESP32 disabled
	// as

n led[3] = sd_d[3]; //led(3) <= sd_d(3); -- sd_d(3) is sd_cs, pullup=NONE in constraints otherwise SD card will prevents esp32 from entering programming mode...
	// ass

gn led[2] = sd_d[2]; //led(2) <= sd_d(2);
	// assign led[1] = sd_d[1]; //led(1) <= sd_d(1);

f user presses BTN0 and BTN1 then pull down PROGRAMN for multiboot
  a

Tags.HoverText: The reserved word input is a port definition providing a means of interconnecting hardware descriptions consisting of modules, primitives, and macromodules. For example, module X can instantiate module Y, using port connections appropriate to module X. These port names can differ from the names of the internal nets and registers specified in the definition of module Y.

Wire nets connect elements. The net-type wire is identical in syntax and function to the net-type tri. A wire net can be used for nets that are driven by a single gate or continuous assignment. Logical conflicts from multiple sources on a wire net result in unknown values unless the net is controlled by logic strength.

Tags.HoverText: The assign statement is used to express the first type of procedural continuous assignment. Assign of reg data types is not supported.

Wire nets connect elements. The net-type wire is identical in syntax and function to the net-type tri. A wire net can be used for nets that are driven by a single gate or continuous assignment. Logical conflicts from multiple sources on a wire net result in unknown values unless the net is controlled by logic strength.

A reg is an abstraction of a data storage element. The reg stores a value from one assignment to the next. An assignment statement in a procedure acts as a trigger that changes the value in the data storage element. Reg data types can only be assigned inside of the procedural, initial, always blocks or when they are declared.

Value changes on nets and registers can be used as events to trigger the execution of a statement. This is known as detecting an implicit event. The event can be based on the direction of the change towards the value 1 (posedge) or towards the value 0 (negedge).
